rule_id: # Only one primary rhythm label survives; highest Pai wins.
# Tier-1 labels (VF
rule_type: VT) are NEVER suppressed regardless of Pai.

rule_id: R1
# ── Precedence: critical rhythms override lower-acuity ones ──────────────────
# VF overrides everything (cardiac arrest = no other rhythm relevant)
R2a
# VT overrides sinus rhythms (but not AF — both can coexist in certain presentations)
R2b
# Complete AV block overrides lower-degree blocks
R2c
# Mobitz II overrides Mobitz I and 1st degree
R2d
# ── Derived: STEMI from ST elevation pattern ─────────────────────────────────
# primary_label = trigger (must be in model output)
# related_labels[0] = diagnosis to derive or boost
# If ST_Elevation detected AND chest_pain present → derive/boost STEMI
R3a
# If ST_Elevation detected WITHOUT chest pain → more likely pericarditis
R3b
# ── Derived: NSTEMI / UA from ST depression pattern ──────────────────────────
# ST depression + chest pain → NSTEMI
R4a
# ST depression without chest pain → possible UA
R4b
# ── Derived: AF from irregular rhythm pattern ─────────────────────────────────
R5
# ── Derived: conduction blocks from wide QRS pattern ─────────────────────────
# Wide QRS + no structural context → evaluate for LBBB or RBBB
R6a
R6b
# ── Derived: LBBB + ACS = STEMI-equivalent (Sgarbossa) ──────────────────────
# New LBBB with chest pain is treated as STEMI-equivalent per AHA 2013
R7
# Reduce AF urgency if completely asymptomatic (e.g. incidental finding)
R8a
# Reduce SVT urgency if asymptomatic
R8b
# Reduce Sinus_Bradycardia urgency if asymptomatic (athlete's bradycardia)
R8c
# Reduce LBBB urgency if no chest pain and no CAD risk (chronic
R8d
# Reduce NSTEMI score if young patient with no cardiac risk factors
R8e
# ── Safety: flag STEMI + LVH_Strain co-occurrence as possible mimic ──────────
# LVH strain causes lateral ST depression that can reduce STEMI specificity
# NOTE: STEMI allow_downgrade=FALSE so score reduction is blocked — this is intentional.
# The co-occurrence is still surfaced in the active_results list for the clinician.
R9
